{{Infobox Infobox ancient site |name=Shiratorizuka Kofun |image=[[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |caption=Overall view (foreground: posterior circular mound; background: anterior rectangular mound) |location={{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|35|15|18.67|N|137|02|39.63|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |shape=Keyhole-shaped kofun |era=Late 4th century |size=Overall length: 115m
Width of anterior mound: 40m
Diameter of posterior mound: 75m |artifacts={{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, haji ware shards, and others |designation=National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |map=Japan Aichi#Nagoya |icon=前方後円墳-南西 }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Posterior circular mound]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Group|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. This kofun is the third-largest in Aichi PrefectureOn-site informational plaque. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == Shiratorizuka Kofun is shaped like a hand mirror and lacks evidence of haniwa, leading scholars to date its construction to the late 4th century. It is said that the mound was named after its use of white silica stones (keiishi) on the summit of its posterior mound. These stones were part of the fukiishi that covered the mound's slopes. Historical maps from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} label the site as "Shiratoriyama," suggesting that many of these stones remained intact during that era. Today, only small fragments of fukiishi can be found. During the Taishō era, the summit of the posterior mound collapsed over an area of 1.8 meters square. In the early Shōwa period, local historian Yoshitaka Hasegawa conducted an excavation of this area, but no burial facilities or grave goods were documented. The mound was later backfilled, leaving a slight depression visible today. After World War II, urban development threatened the site, but efforts by local stakeholders preserved it. In 1972, it was designated a National Historic Site[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofun Group Preservation Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10–11.. In 2014, the site's designation was expanded to include six additional kofun, such as the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, under the collective name "Shidami Kofun Group."October 6, 2014 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 140. Reference: {{PDFlink|[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Group Designated as a National Historic Site"]}} (Nagoya City website). According to local legend preserved in the {{ill|Tōshunki|ja|東春日井郡誌}}, Shiratorizuka Kofun is linked to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}. The story recounts that Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake near Ibukiyama and, while washing the wound in a river, encountered a white bird. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari Province. The bird flew to the base of Mount Higashiyama, where it died, leading Yamato Takeru to construct a tomb for it, known as Shiratorizuka. == Dimensions and Structure == * Total length: 115 meters * Anterior rectangular mound: ''' Length: 43 meters ''' Height: 6.7 meters (north side) / 5 meters (south side) ''' Width: 40 meters * Narrowest point: 25 meters * Posterior circular mound: ''' Diameter: 75 meters ''' Height: 15.2 meters (north side) / 12.6 meters (south side) == Surveys == A 1969 survey by Nagoya University Archaeology Lab recorded the kofun's dimensions as 109 meters in length, 71 meters in diameter for the posterior mound, and 14 meters in height for the posterior mound, with the anterior mound measuring 40 meters wide and 5 meters high. However, investigations in 2005–2006 revealed slightly larger dimensions. Researchers speculate that the anterior mound has two tiers, the posterior mound three tiers, and that additional projections may have existed at various points. Geological surveys suggest the presence of burial facilities oriented north-south, potentially aligned east-west in parallel. In 1992, an excavation by the Nagoya City Board of Education identified a remaining moat on the southeastern side of the anterior mound. In 2008, this moat's presence led to an expansion of the designated Historic Site area. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} * Bowls with lids * Pottery shards (土師器) * Other pottery, including jars (甕) == Photo Gallery == File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering from the southeast File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering from the northwest File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 5 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering from the front of the anterior mound File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering from the posterior mound File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 7 7p.jpg|3D rendering from the side == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央線}}, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ''' From either station, take the {{ill|Yutorito Line|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} to "Higashiyabashi" bus stop. == References == * {{small|''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) – Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963.}} * {{small|''National Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun Investigation Report'' – Miharashidai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007.}} * {{small|''Shinshu Nagoya-shi-shi Archaeology Vol. 1'' – Nagoya City, 2008.}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[ja:[[Category:Kofun in Nagoya]] [[ja:[[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[ja:[[Category:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[ja:[[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[ja:[[Category:Shidami Kofun Group]] [[ja:[[Category:Kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)]][[category:original pages]] [[ja:[[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]] [[ja:[[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市守山区)]]

{{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}}}} {{Infobox ancient site |Name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} |Latitude_deg = 35 |Latitude_min = 15 |Latitude_sec = 18.67 |Longitude_deg = 137 |Longitude_min = 02 |Longitude_sec = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_period = Late 4th century |Dimensions = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |Artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}} [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that form part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was designated as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Tumulus|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially designated as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historical Site. == Dimensions and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' - Length: 43m - Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) - Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' - Diameter: 75m - Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger dimensions. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) - From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}}}} {{Infobox ancient site |Name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} |Latitude_deg = 35 |Latitude_min = 15 |Latitude_sec = 18.67 |Longitude_deg = 137 |Longitude_min = 02 |Longitude_sec = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_period = Late 4th century |Dimensions = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |Artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that form part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was designated as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Tumulus|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially designated as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historical Site. == Dimensions and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' - Length: 43m - Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) - Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' - Diameter: 75m - Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger dimensions. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) - From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] revision.text:
{{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}}}} {{Infobox ancient site |Name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} |Latitude_deg = 35 |Latitude_min = 15 |Latitude_sec = 18.67 |Longitude_deg = 137 |Longitude_min = 02 |Longitude_sec = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_period = Late 4th century |Dimensions = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |Artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that form part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was designated as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Tumulus|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially designated as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historical Site. == Dimensions and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' - Length: 43m - Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) - Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' - Diameter: 75m - Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger dimensions. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) - From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shiratori-zuka Kofun (Nagoya)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] revision.comment: fixing parameters
revision.text:
{{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}}}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} |Latitude_deg = 35 |Latitude_min = 15 |Latitude_sec = 18.67 |Longitude_deg = 137 |Longitude_min = 02 |Longitude_sec = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_period = Late 4th century |Dimensions = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |Artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that form part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was designated as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Tumulus|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially designated as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historical Site. == Dimensions and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' - Length: 43m - Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) - Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' - Diameter: 75m - Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger dimensions. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) - From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}}}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} |Latitude_deg = 35 |Latitude_min = 15 |Latitude_sec = 18.67 |Longitude_deg = 137 |Longitude_min = 02 |Longitude_sec = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_period = Late 4th century |Dimensions = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |Artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that form part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was designated as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Tumulus|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially designated as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historical Site. == Dimensions and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' - Length: 43m - Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) - Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' - Diameter: 75m - Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger dimensions. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) - From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) - From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] revision.comment: Adding [[Category:dd]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}}}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} |Latitude_deg = 35 |Latitude_min = 15 |Latitude_sec = 18.67 |Longitude_deg = 137 |Longitude_min = 02 |Longitude_sec = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_period = Late 4th century |Dimensions = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |Artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that form part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was designated as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Tumulus|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially designated as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historical Site. == Dimensions and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' - Length: 43m - Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) - Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' - Diameter: 75m - Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger dimensions. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) - From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]] ==References==

Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was designated as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Tumulus|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially designated as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historical Site. == Dimensions and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' - Length: 43m - Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) - Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' - Diameter: 75m - Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger dimensions. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) - From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}}}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} |Latitude_deg = 35 |Latitude_min = 15 |Latitude_sec = 18.67 |Longitude_deg = 137 |Longitude_min = 02 |Longitude_sec = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_period = Late 4th century |Dimensions = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |Artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that form part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was designated as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Tumulus|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially designated as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historical Site. == Dimensions and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' - Length: 43m - Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) - Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' - Diameter: 75m - Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger dimensions. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) - From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}}}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} |Latitude_deg = 35 |Latitude_min = 15 |Latitude_sec = 18.67 |Longitude_deg = 137 |Longitude_min = 02 |Longitude_sec = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_period = Late 4th century |Dimensions = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |Artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that form part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was designated as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Tumulus|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially designated as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historical Site. == Dimensions and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' - Length: 43m - Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) - Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' - Diameter: 75m - Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger dimensions. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) - From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}}}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} |Latitude_deg = 35 |Latitude_min = 15 |Latitude_sec = 18.67 |Longitude_deg = 137 |Longitude_min = 02 |Longitude_sec = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_period = Late 4th century |Dimensions = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |Artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that form part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was designated as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Tumulus|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially designated as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historical Site. == Dimensions and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' - Length: 43m - Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) - Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' - Diameter: 75m - Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger dimensions. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) - From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}}}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} |Latitude_deg = 35 |Latitude_min = 15 |Latitude_sec = 18.67 |Longitude_deg = 137 |Longitude_min = 02 |Longitude_sec = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_period = Late 4th century |Dimensions = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |Artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that form part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was designated as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Tumulus|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially designated as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historical Site. == Dimensions and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' - Length: 43m - Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) - Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' - Diameter: 75m - Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger dimensions. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) - From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}}}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} |Latitude_deg = 35 |Latitude_min = 15 |Latitude_sec = 18.67 |Longitude_deg = 137 |Longitude_min = 02 |Longitude_sec = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_period = Late 4th century |Dimensions = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |Artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that form part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was designated as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Tumulus|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially designated as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historical Site. == Dimensions and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' - Length: 43m - Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) - Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' - Diameter: 75m - Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger dimensions. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) - From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |Artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |Map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that form part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was designated as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Tumulus|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially designated as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historical Site. == Dimensions and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' - Length: 43m - Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) - Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' - Diameter: 75m - Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger dimensions. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) - From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』-
