So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} |coordinates_deg = 35 |coordinates_min = 15 |coordinates_sec = 18.67 |coordinates_deg = 137 |coordinates_min = 02 |coordinates_sec = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction_period = Late 4th century |area = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National other_designation (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that form part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is designated as a National other_designation of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was designated as a National other_designation on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially designated as a National other_designation"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster other_designation. == area and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} |coordinates_deg = 35 |coordinates_min = 15 |coordinates_sec = 18.67 |coordinates_deg = 137 |coordinates_min = 02 |coordinates_sec = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction_period = Late 4th century |area = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National other_designation (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that form part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is designation1 as a National other_designation of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was designation1 as a National other_designation on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially designation1 as a National other_designation"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster other_designation. == area and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger dimensions. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15 |coordinates = 18.67 |coordinates = 137 |coordinates = 02 |coordinates = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |built = Late 4th century |area = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National other_designation (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that form part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is designation1 as a National other_designation of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was designation1 as a National other_designation on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially designation1 as a National other_designation"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster other_designation. == area and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger dimensions. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shiratorizuka Kofun This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15 |coordinates = 18.67 |coordinates = 137 |coordinates = 02 |coordinates = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |built = Late 4th century |area = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National other_designation (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that form part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is designation1 as a National other_designation of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was designation1 as a National other_designation on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially designation1 as a National other_designation"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster other_designation. == area and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger area. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15 |coordinates = 18.67 |coordinates = 137 |coordinates = 02 |coordinates = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |built = Late 4th century |area = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National other_designation (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that form part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is designation1 as a National other_designation of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was designation1 as a National other_designation on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially designation1 as a National other_designation"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster other_designation. == area and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger area. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15 |coordinates = 18.67 |coordinates = 137 |coordinates = 02 |coordinates = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |built = Late 4th century |area = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National other_other_designation (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that type part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is other_designation1 as a National other_other_designation of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was other_designation1 as a National other_other_designation on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the other_designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially other_designation1 as a National other_other_designation"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster other_other_designation. == area and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger area. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shiratorizuka Kofun But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15 |coordinates = 18.67 |coordinates = 137 |coordinates = 02 |coordinates = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |built = Late 4th century |area = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National other_other_designation (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that type part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is other_designation1 as a National other_other_designation of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was other_designation1 as a National other_other_designation on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the other_designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially other_designation1 as a National other_other_designation"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster other_other_designation. == area and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger area. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shiratorizuka Kofun {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15 |coordinates = 18.67 |coordinates = 137 |coordinates = 02 |coordinates = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |built = Late 4th century |area = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National other_other_designation (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that type part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is other_designation1 as a National other_other_designation of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was other_designation1 as a National other_other_designation on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the other_designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially other_designation1 as a National other_other_designation"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster other_other_designation. == area and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. pottery}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15 |coordinates = 18.67 |coordinates = 137 |coordinates = 02 |coordinates = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |built = Late 4th century |area = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National other_other_designation (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that type part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is other_designation1 as a National other_other_designation of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was other_designation1 as a National other_other_designation on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the other_designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially other_designation1 as a National other_other_designation"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster other_other_designation. == area and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger area. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15 |coordinates = 18.67 |coordinates = 137 |coordinates = 02 |coordinates = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |built = Late 4th century |area = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National other_designation (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that type part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is other_designation1 as a National other_designation of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was other_designation1 as a National other_designation on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the other_designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially other_designation1 as a National other_designation"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster other_designation. == area and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger area. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shiratorizuka Kofun Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National other_designation (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is one of the tumuli that type part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} and is other_designation1 as a National other_designation of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was other_designation1 as a National other_designation on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳}}, were incorporated into the other_designation, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially other_designation1 as a National other_designation"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster other_designation. == area and Structure == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger area. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun
