revision.comment: Text replacement - "|12=draft" to "|qq=draft"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15 |coordinates = 18.67 |coordinates = 137 |coordinates = 02 |coordinates = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |built = Late 4th century |area = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is one of the tumuli that type part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, were incorporated into the Historic Site, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historic Site. == area and burial == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger area. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ==References== {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Shiratori-zuka Kofun (Nagoya)}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagoya]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Owari clan|burial Shiratorizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Shidami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)]] revision.comment: 42 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市) up to rev 88671607
revision.text:
{{otheruses|名古屋市守山区にある[[志段味古墳群]]中の古墳|志段味古墳群中の別の一群の古墳|白鳥古墳群|名古屋市[[熱田区]]にある古墳|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 白鳥塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 全景（左奥に前方部、右手前に後円部） |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]]上志段味字東谷2107番地 |所属 = [[志段味古墳群]] | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 15|緯度秒 = 18.67 | 経度度 = 137|経度分 = 02|経度秒 = 39.63 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |築造年代 = [[4世紀]]後半 |規模 = 墳丘長115[[メートル|m]]
前方部幅40m
後円部径75m |出土品 = [[須恵器]]・坏蓋・[[土師器]]片など |史跡指定 = 国の[[史跡]]「白鳥塚古墳」
（「[[志段味古墳群]]」に包含） |地図 = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南西 }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[3DCG]]で描画した白鳥塚古墳}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|後円部]] '''白鳥塚古墳'''（しらとりづかこふん）は、[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]]にある[[前方後円墳]]。[[志段味古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「[[志段味古墳群]]」のうち）。 [[愛知県]]では3番目の大きさの古墳で現地説明板。、[[4世紀]]後半の築造とされる。 == 概要 == 手鏡型[[前方後円墳]]で、[[埴輪]]が出土していないことなども合わせて[[4世紀]]後半の築造と考えられている。かつて墳丘の斜面は河原石の[[葺石]]で覆われており、特に後円部の頂上部の葺石には白色[[珪石]]が使用されていたことから白鳥塚と呼ばれるようになったと言われている。[[江戸時代]]の絵図には「白鳥山」としてその名を記されており、その時期までは多くの葺石が残っていた可能性もあるが、現在では小片がわずかに見られるのみである。[[大正]]年間に後円部の墳頂が 1.8[[メートル]]四方に渡って陥没。[[昭和]]初期に郷土史家の長谷川佳隆によりその部分の[[発掘調査]]が行われたが、その際に埋葬施設や[[副葬品]]が確認されたとの記録は残されていない。なお、調査後に埋め戻された後円部の墳頂は若干へこんだ状態で、現在でもその範囲を見て取れる。 [[第二次世界大戦|第二次大戦]]後に周辺開発が進んだ際には私有地にあった同古墳の一部の土地が売却されたこともあったが、関係者の尽力により破壊を免れて現在に至る。[[1972年]]（[[昭和]]47年）[[11月6日]]に国の[[史跡]]に指定された[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf 「史跡志段味古墳群保存管理計画」]、名古屋市教育委員会、2015、pp.10 - 11。なお、[[2014年]]（[[平成]]26年）10月6日付で史跡「白鳥塚古墳」に[[尾張戸神社|尾張戸神社古墳]]など6基を追加指定し、指定名称を「[[志段味古墳群]]」に改めた平成26年10月6日[[文部科学省]]告示第140号（参照：{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf 「志段味古墳群が正式に国史跡に指定されました」]}}（名古屋市ホームページ）。。 この古墳は[[ヤマトタケル]]伝説があり、『東春日井郡誌』によるとヤマトタケルが[[伊吹山]]の賊を征伐に行く途中に蛇に足を噛まれ、傷口を川で足を洗っていたところ、一羽の白鳥が現れた。ヤマトタケルが白鳥に尾張に連れて行ってくれと頼み、ヤマトタケルを乗せた白鳥が尾張の東谷山の麓に着いたところで死んだため、ヤマトタケルがその場所に白鳥の墓を建てその墓が白鳥塚だと伝わっている[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html 歴史の里　しだみ古墳群 しだみ古墳群の古墳 白鳥塚古墳（しらとりづかこふん）]。 == 規模と構造 == * 墳丘全長：115メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：43メートル ** 高さ：6.7メートル（北側）/ 5メートル（南側） ** 幅：40メートル * くびれ部幅：25メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：75メートル ** 高さ：15.2メートル（北側）/ 12.6メートル（南側） == 各種調査 == [[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）の[[名古屋大学]]考古学研究室による測量調査により、全長109メートル、後円部径71メートル、後円部高さ14メートル、前方部幅40メートル、前方部高5メートルとされてきたが、[[2005年]]（[[平成]]17年）と翌年の調査によって、それよりも若干規模が大きいことが確認された。前方部は2段、後円部は3段築成と推定され、前方部端と北側、後円部の各1か所以上に[[造り出し]]があった可能性も指摘されている。前方部の一部は川に面した崖状になっており歪な形状だが、築造当初からこの形状であったのか、後の侵食によるものなのかはわかっていない。さらに[[物理探査]]の結果、南北方向に置かれた埋葬施設が東西に並列している可能性もあるとされる。 また、[[1992年]]（[[平成]]14年）の[[名古屋市教育委員会]]による範囲確認・掘削調査により前方部南東側に[[周濠]]が残存することが判明しており、[[2008年]]（[[平成]]20年）に史跡の指定区域が追加された。 == 写真集 == File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。南東方向から見る File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。北西方向から見る File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 5 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。前方部正面から見る File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 6 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。後円部方向から見る File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 7 7p.jpg|3DCGで描画。真横から見る == 出土品 == * [[須恵器]] * 坏蓋 * 𤭯（はそう、漢字は左が「瓦」、右が「泉」） * [[甕]] * [[土師器]]片 == 交通アクセス == * [[大曽根駅]]（[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[中央線 (名古屋地区)|中央本線]]、[[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄瀬戸線|瀬戸線]]、[[名古屋市営地下鉄]][[名古屋市営地下鉄名城線|名城線]]） * [[高蔵寺駅]]（JR中央本線、[[愛知環状鉄道]]・[[愛知環状鉄道線]]）より ** [[名古屋ガイドウェイバス]][[名古屋ガイドウェイバスガイドウェイバス志段味線|ガイドウェイバス志段味線]]（[[ゆとりーとライン]]）「東谷橋」バス停下車。 == 参考文献 == * 『守山の古墳』- 守山市教育委員会、昭和38年（[[1963年]]） * 『国指定白鳥塚古墳 第1次〜第3次範囲確認調査概要報告書』− 見晴台考古資料館・編、名古屋市教育委員会・発行、平成19年（[[2007年]]） * 『[[新修名古屋市史]] 資料編 考古1』- 新修名古屋市史史料編編集委員会・編、名古屋市・発行、平成20年（[[2008年]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[白鳥古墳群]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:しらとりつか こふん}} [[Category:名古屋市の古墳]] [[Category:愛知県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:守山区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:尾張氏|墓しらとりつかこふん]] [[Category:志段味古墳群]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]] to [[Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{otheruses|名古屋市守山区にある[[志段味古墳群]]中の古墳|志段味古墳群中の別の一群の古墳|白鳥古墳群|名古屋市[[熱田区]]にある古墳|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 白鳥塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 全景（左奥に前方部、右手前に後円部） |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]]上志段味字東谷2107番地 |所属 = [[志段味古墳群]] | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 15|緯度秒 = 18.67 | 経度度 = 137|経度分 = 02|経度秒 = 39.63 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |築造年代 = [[4世紀]]後半 |規模 = 墳丘長115[[メートル|m]]
前方部幅40m
後円部径75m |出土品 = [[須恵器]]・坏蓋・[[土師器]]片など |史跡指定 = 国の[[史跡]]「白鳥塚古墳」
（「[[志段味古墳群]]」に包含） |地図 = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南西 }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[3DCG]]で描画した白鳥塚古墳}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|後円部]] '''白鳥塚古墳'''（しらとりづかこふん）は、[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]]にある[[前方後円墳]]。[[志段味古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「[[志段味古墳群]]」のうち）。 [[愛知県]]では3番目の大きさの古墳で現地説明板。、[[4世紀]]後半の築造とされる。 == 概要 == 手鏡型[[前方後円墳]]で、[[埴輪]]が出土していないことなども合わせて[[4世紀]]後半の築造と考えられている。かつて墳丘の斜面は河原石の[[葺石]]で覆われており、特に後円部の頂上部の葺石には白色[[珪石]]が使用されていたことから白鳥塚と呼ばれるようになったと言われている。[[江戸時代]]の絵図には「白鳥山」としてその名を記されており、その時期までは多くの葺石が残っていた可能性もあるが、現在では小片がわずかに見られるのみである。[[大正]]年間に後円部の墳頂が 1.8[[メートル]]四方に渡って陥没。[[昭和]]初期に郷土史家の長谷川佳隆によりその部分の[[発掘調査]]が行われたが、その際に埋葬施設や[[副葬品]]が確認されたとの記録は残されていない。なお、調査後に埋め戻された後円部の墳頂は若干へこんだ状態で、現在でもその範囲を見て取れる。 [[第二次世界大戦|第二次大戦]]後に周辺開発が進んだ際には私有地にあった同古墳の一部の土地が売却されたこともあったが、関係者の尽力により破壊を免れて現在に至る。[[1972年]]（[[昭和]]47年）[[11月6日]]に国の[[史跡]]に指定された[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf 「史跡志段味古墳群保存管理計画」]、名古屋市教育委員会、2015、pp.10 - 11。なお、[[2014年]]（[[平成]]26年）10月6日付で史跡「白鳥塚古墳」に[[尾張戸神社|尾張戸神社古墳]]など6基を追加指定し、指定名称を「[[志段味古墳群]]」に改めた平成26年10月6日[[文部科学省]]告示第140号（参照：{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf 「志段味古墳群が正式に国史跡に指定されました」]}}（名古屋市ホームページ）。。 この古墳は[[ヤマトタケル]]伝説があり、『東春日井郡誌』によるとヤマトタケルが[[伊吹山]]の賊を征伐に行く途中に蛇に足を噛まれ、傷口を川で足を洗っていたところ、一羽の白鳥が現れた。ヤマトタケルが白鳥に尾張に連れて行ってくれと頼み、ヤマトタケルを乗せた白鳥が尾張の東谷山の麓に着いたところで死んだため、ヤマトタケルがその場所に白鳥の墓を建てその墓が白鳥塚だと伝わっている[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html 歴史の里　しだみ古墳群 しだみ古墳群の古墳 白鳥塚古墳（しらとりづかこふん）]。 == 規模と構造 == * 墳丘全長：115メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：43メートル ** 高さ：6.7メートル（北側）/ 5メートル（南側） ** 幅：40メートル * くびれ部幅：25メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：75メートル ** 高さ：15.2メートル（北側）/ 12.6メートル（南側） == 各種調査 == [[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）の[[名古屋大学]]考古学研究室による測量調査により、全長109メートル、後円部径71メートル、後円部高さ14メートル、前方部幅40メートル、前方部高5メートルとされてきたが、[[2005年]]（[[平成]]17年）と翌年の調査によって、それよりも若干規模が大きいことが確認された。前方部は2段、後円部は3段築成と推定され、前方部端と北側、後円部の各1か所以上に[[造り出し]]があった可能性も指摘されている。前方部の一部は川に面した崖状になっており歪な形状だが、築造当初からこの形状であったのか、後の侵食によるものなのかはわかっていない。さらに[[物理探査]]の結果、南北方向に置かれた埋葬施設が東西に並列している可能性もあるとされる。 また、[[1992年]]（[[平成]]14年）の[[名古屋市教育委員会]]による範囲確認・掘削調査により前方部南東側に[[周濠]]が残存することが判明しており、[[2008年]]（[[平成]]20年）に史跡の指定区域が追加された。 == 写真集 == File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。南東方向から見る File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。北西方向から見る File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 5 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。前方部正面から見る File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 6 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。後円部方向から見る File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 7 7p.jpg|3DCGで描画。真横から見る == 出土品 == * [[須恵器]] * 坏蓋 * 𤭯（はそう、漢字は左が「瓦」、右が「泉」） * [[甕]] * [[土師器]]片 == 交通アクセス == * [[大曽根駅]]（[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[中央線 (名古屋地区)|中央本線]]、[[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄瀬戸線|瀬戸線]]、[[名古屋市営地下鉄]][[名古屋市営地下鉄名城線|名城線]]） * [[高蔵寺駅]]（JR中央本線、[[愛知環状鉄道]]・[[愛知環状鉄道線]]）より ** [[名古屋ガイドウェイバス]][[名古屋ガイドウェイバスガイドウェイバス志段味線|ガイドウェイバス志段味線]]（[[ゆとりーとライン]]）「東谷橋」バス停下車。 == 参考文献 == * 『守山の古墳』- 守山市教育委員会、昭和38年（[[1963年]]） * 『国指定白鳥塚古墳 第1次〜第3次範囲確認調査概要報告書』− 見晴台考古資料館・編、名古屋市教育委員会・発行、平成19年（[[2007年]]） * 『[[新修名古屋市史]] 資料編 考古1』- 新修名古屋市史史料編編集委員会・編、名古屋市・発行、平成20年（[[2008年]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[白鳥古墳群]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:しらとりつか こふん}} [[Category:名古屋市の古墳]] [[Category:愛知県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:守山区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:尾張氏|墓しらとりつかこふん]] revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15 |coordinates = 18.67 |coordinates = 137 |coordinates = 02 |coordinates = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |built = Late 4th century |area = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is one of the tumuli that type part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, were incorporated into the Historic Site, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historic Site. == area and burial == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger area. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ==References== {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Shiratori-zuka Kofun (Nagoya)}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15 |coordinates = 18.67 |coordinates = 137 |coordinates = 02 |coordinates = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |built = Late 4th century |area = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is one of the tumuli that type part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, were incorporated into the Historic Site, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historic Site. == area and burial == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger area. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ==References== {{citation needed|Shiratori-zuka Kofun (Nagoya)}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|version=88671607|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] Prefecture]] [[Category:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Owari clan|burial Shiratorizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Shidami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratorizuka Kofun Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is one of the tumuli that type part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, were incorporated into the Historic Site, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historic Site. == area and burial == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger area. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|version=88671607|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)]][[sn:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)]]}} [[sn:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)]] [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]]

Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ibuki|qq=}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historic Site. == area and burial == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger area. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzone Station|qq=}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzōji Station|qq=}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Infobox ancient site|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|version=88671607|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)]] Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Cluster|qq=}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya|qq=}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|qq=}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15 |coordinates = 18.67 |coordinates = 137 |coordinates = 02 |coordinates = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} |built = Late 4th century |area = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|qq=}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}. It is one of the tumuli that type part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owarito Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owarito Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, were incorporated into the Historic Site, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ibuki|qq=}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historic Site. == area and burial == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger area. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzone Station|qq=}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzōji Station|qq=}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Infobox ancient site|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|version=88671607|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)]] Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzone Station|qq=}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzōji Station|qq=}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Infobox ancient site|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|version=88671607|comment=Imported full Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Cluster|qq=}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya|qq=}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|qq=}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15 |coordinates = 18.67 |coordinates = 137 |coordinates = 02 |coordinates = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} |built = Late 4th century |area = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|qq=}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}. It is one of the tumuli that type part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owari-to Shrine|lt=Owari-to Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社|lt_ja=尾張戸神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owarito Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, were incorporated into the Historic Site, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ibuki|qq=}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historic Site. == area and burial == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger area. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzone Station|qq=}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzōji Station|qq=}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Infobox ancient site|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|version=88671607|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)]] So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Cluster|qq=}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya|qq=}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|qq=}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15 |coordinates = 18.67 |coordinates = 137 |coordinates = 02 |coordinates = 39.63 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} |built = Late 4th century |area = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|ja|守山区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|qq=}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}. It is one of the tumuli that type part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owari-to Shrine|lt=Owari-to Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社|lt_ja=尾張戸神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owarito Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, were incorporated into the Historic Site, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|ja|伊吹山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ibuki|qq=}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historic Site. == area and burial == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger area. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzone Station|qq=}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzōji Station|qq=}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Infobox ancient site|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|version=88671607|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagoya]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Owari clan|burial Shiratorizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Shidami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)]][[sn:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)]]}} [[sn:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)]] [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]]
