revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580550]] Wikidata name: Shiratorizuka-kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|lt=Shiratori Kofun Group|lt=Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Cluster|qq=}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.255186|N|137.044342|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |built = Late 4th century |area = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is one of the tumuli that type part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster}} and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owari-to Shrine|lt=Owari-to Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社|lt_ja=尾張戸神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owarito Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, were incorporated into the Historic Site, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|en|Mount Ibuki|WD=Q2624360|lt=Mount Ibuki}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historic Site. == area and burial == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger area. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|en|Ōzone Station|WD=Q872075|lt=Ōzone Station}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|en|Kōzōji Station|WD=Q5359256|lt=Kōzōji Station}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Infobox ancient site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|lt=Shiratori Kofun Group|lt=Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|version=88671607|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{Commons category|Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11580550}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagoya]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Owari clan|burial Shiratorizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Shidami Kofun group]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|lt=Shiratori Kofun Group|lt=Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Cluster|qq=}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.255186|N|137.044342|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |built = Late 4th century |area = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is one of the tumuli that type part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster}} and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owari-to Shrine|lt=Owari-to Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社|lt_ja=尾張戸神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owarito Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, were incorporated into the Historic Site, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|en|Mount Ibuki|WD=Q2624360|lt=Mount Ibuki}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historic Site. == area and burial == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger area. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|en|Ōzone Station|WD=Q872075|lt=Ōzone Station}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|en|Kōzōji Station|WD=Q5359256|lt=Kōzōji Station}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Infobox ancient site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|lt=Shiratori Kofun Group|lt=Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|version=88671607|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{Commons category|Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11580550}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagoya]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Owari clan|burial Shiratorizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Shidami Kofun group]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|en|Mount Ibuki|WD=Q2624360|lt=Mount Ibuki|1=Mount Ibuki}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historic Site. == area and burial == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger area. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|en|Ōzone Station|WD=Q872075|lt=Ōzone Station|1=Ōzone Station}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|en|Kōzōji Station|WD=Q5359256|lt=Kōzōji Station|1=Kōzōji Station}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Infobox ancient site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|lt=Shiratori Kofun Group|lt=Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|version=88671607|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{Commons category|Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11580550}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagoya]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Owari clan|burial Shiratorizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Shidami Kofun group]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster|1=Shidami Kofun group}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|lt=Shiratori Kofun Group|lt=Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Cluster|qq=}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster|1=Shidami Kofun group}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.255186|N|137.044342|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |built = Late 4th century |area = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster|1=Shidami Kofun group}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is one of the tumuli that type part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster|1=Shidami Kofun group}} and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owari-to Shrine|lt=Owari-to Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社|lt_ja=尾張戸神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owarito Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, were incorporated into the Historic Site, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|en|Mount Ibuki|WD=Q2624360|lt=Mount Ibuki|1=Mount Ibuki}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historic Site. == area and burial == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger area. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|en|Ōzone Station|WD=Q872075|lt=Ōzone Station|1=Ōzone Station}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|en|Kōzōji Station|WD=Q5359256|lt=Kōzōji Station|1=Kōzōji Station}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Infobox ancient site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|lt=Shiratori Kofun Group|lt=Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|version=88671607|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{Commons category|Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11580550}} [[Category:History of Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagoya]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Owari clan|burial Shiratorizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Shidami Kofun group]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{otheruses|A tumulus within the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster|1=Shidami Kofun group}} in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another group of tumuli within the Shidami Kofun Cluster|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|lt=Shiratori Kofun Group|lt=Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Cluster|qq=}}|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|{{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Shiratorizuka Kofun (7), Togoku Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|280px]] |caption = Full view (the front square section on the left, the rear circular section on the right) |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}}, Kamishidami Azuma no Tani 2107 |Cluster = {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster|1=Shidami Kofun group}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.255186|N|137.044342|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |built = Late 4th century |area = Total length: 115m
Front section width: 40m
Rear section diameter: 75m |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, plates, fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, etc. |Cultural_property_status = National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster|1=Shidami Kofun group}}) |map = Japan Aichi#Nagoya |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - southwest |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:CG Shiratoriduka kofun 8 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|3D rendering of Shiratorizuka Kofun}}]] [[File:Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya) koen.JPG|thumb|250px|right|The rear circular section]] '''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is one of the tumuli that type part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster|1=Shidami Kofun group}} and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site of Japan. It is the third-largest tumulus in Aichi PrefectureOn-site explanatory sign. and is believed to have been constructed in the late 4th century. == Overview == The tumulus has a hand-mirror-shaped keyhole design. Its lack of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} suggests it was built in the late 4th century. The slopes of the tumulus were once covered with river stones as {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}, and the apex of the rear circular section used white quartzite stones, which is said to have led to its name, "Shiratorizuka" ("White Bird Mound"). Historical maps from the Edo period list it as "Shiratoriyama," indicating that many of the fukiishi may have been intact until that time. Currently, only small fragments of the fukiishi remain visible. During the Taishō period, a 1.8-meter square depression appeared at the apex of the rear circular section. An excavation was conducted in the early Shōwa period by local historian Hasegawa Yoshitaka, but no records of burial facilities or grave goods were left. The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owari-to Shrine|lt=Owari-to Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社|lt_ja=尾張戸神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owarito Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, were incorporated into the Historic Site, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|en|Mount Ibuki|WD=Q2624360|lt=Mount Ibuki|1=Mount Ibuki}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historic Site. == area and burial == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger area. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|en|Ōzone Station|WD=Q872075|lt=Ōzone Station|1=Ōzone Station}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|en|Kōzōji Station|WD=Q5359256|lt=Kōzōji Station|1=Kōzōji Station}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Infobox ancient site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|lt=Shiratori Kofun Group|lt=Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|version=88671607|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{Commons category|Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11580550}} [[Category:History of Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagoya]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Owari clan|burial Shiratorizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Shidami Kofun group]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

The area was later refilled, leaving a slight depression still visible today. Efforts by local stakeholders have preserved the tumulus from destruction during post-war urban development. It was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site on November 6, 1972.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/pdf/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Preservation Management Plan for Shidami Kofun Cluster"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, pp.10-11 In 2014, six additional tumuli, including the {{ill|Owari-to Shrine|lt=Owari-to Shrine Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社|lt_ja=尾張戸神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owarito Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, were incorporated into the Historic Site, and the site was renamed the "Shidami Kofun Cluster."[http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000063/63862/kunisiseki.pdf "Shidami Kofun Cluster officially Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site"], Nagoya City, 2014. According to legend, the tumulus is connected to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. It is said that while on his way to subjugate bandits on {{ill|Mount Ibuki|en|Mount Ibuki|WD=Q2624360|lt=Mount Ibuki|1=Mount Ibuki}}, Yamato Takeru was bitten by a snake. As he washed his wound in a river, a white bird appeared. Yamato Takeru asked the bird to carry him to Owari, and upon reaching the foot of Higashitani Mountain, the bird died. Yamato Takeru is said to have built the bird's tomb at this location, which became Shiratorizuka.[https://www.rekishinosato.city.nagoya.jp/kofun01.html "Shiratorizuka Kofun"], Shidami Kofun Cluster Historic Site. == area and burial == * '''Total length:''' 115m * '''Front section''' ** Length: 43m ** Height: 6.7m (north) / 5m (south) ** Width: 40m * '''Neck width:''' 25m * '''Rear section''' ** Diameter: 75m ** Height: 15.2m (north) / 12.6m (south) == Excavations == A 1969 survey by the Nagoya University Archaeology Department measured the tumulus at 109m in length with a rear section diameter of 71m and a height of 14m. Later surveys in 2005-2006 confirmed slightly larger area. The tumulus consists of a two-tiered front section and a three-tiered rear section, with potential projections on the front and rear sections. Evidence of moats was also discovered during a 1992 survey. == burial goods == burial goods recovered include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} * Plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} fragments == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|en|Ōzone Station|WD=Q872075|lt=Ōzone Station|1=Ōzone Station}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|en|Kōzōji Station|WD=Q5359256|lt=Kōzōji Station|1=Kōzōji Station}} (JR Chūō Main Line, Aichi Loop Railway) ** From either station, take the Nagoya Guideway Bus Line (Yutorīto Line) and alight at "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == References == * 『Moriyama's Tumuli』- Moriyama City Board of Education, 1963 * 『Nationally Designated Shiratorizuka Kofun First to Third Range Confirmation Reports』- Edited by Miharatani Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Board of Education, 2007 * 『New Nagoya City History, Archaeological Volume 1』- Nagoya City History Editing Committee, 2008 == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Infobox ancient site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|lt=Shiratori Kofun Group|lt=Shiratori Kofun Cluster|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|version=88671607|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:白鳥塚古墳]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{Commons category|Shiratorizuka-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11580550}} [[Category:History of Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagoya]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Owari clan|burial Shiratorizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Shidami Kofun group]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]]
